notes: Acquired by Compaq for $9.6B in June 1998 (then-largest tech acquisition). Compaq acquired by HP in 2002. Alpha processor IP sold to Intel in 2001.

Oracle subsidiary that developed NC software suite. Oracle spent ~$175M on NCI before shutting it down in 1999 and renaming the assets for interactive TV. Foundational to later cloud computing concepts.
Parent of NCI. Oracle remains major enterprise software company; NCI strategy failed but Oracle pivoted successfully to database, cloud (Oracle Cloud).
Licensor of ARM architecture used in DEC's StrongARM. ARM Holdings IPO'd in 2023; ARM architecture now dominates mobile and embedded computing.
Acquired DEC's semiconductor division (including StrongARM) in 1997 as patent lawsuit settlement. NetPC was Intel/Microsoft response to NC threat. Intel later marketed XScale (StrongARM successor).
Co-developer of NetPC with Intel. Microsoft won the 1997-era desktop battle against NC. Later developed Azure cloud, effectively realizing the NC vision.
Early NC market participant; launched IBM Network Station. IBM's NC efforts were modest commercially.
NC market participant with JavaStation. Acquired by Oracle in 2010. Java, Sun's language, was central to NC concept.
